methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: planqc
private.rationale: Same sector (Quantum Computing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Quantum Motion, a Series A quantum computing company founded by John Morton and Simon Benjamin, is evaluated within a competitive landscape of 11 comparable companies—3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private peers (including Quantinuum and Infleqtion). The quantum computing sector remains nascent and heterogeneous, limiting direct peer comparability.

Valuation analysis relies primarily on stage-progression methodology, given insufficient narrow peer data. Using the company's $60M+ total raised as a base proxy, application of a standard 2.0x multiplier for Series A-to-next-stage step-up yields $120M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable; while 7 same-sector comparables exist, none met minimum threshold requirements (≥3 datapoints) within same-stage or adjacent-stage filters with similar valuation tier, rendering sector-wide medians unreliable. The single-method approach constrains confidence.

The triangulated range spans $102M to $138M, centered at $120M, derived conservatively around the stage-progression result. Material uncertainties include the absence of robust peer multiples in quantum computing's early development phase, heterogeneous technology approaches across the comp set (superconducting, trapped-ion, photonic), and pre-commercialization risk profiles that limit comparable trading multiples. Post-Series A funding appetite and technical validation milestones will materially influence realized valuation.
